title: nephrodium subobliquatum
intro: Nephrodium subobliquatum, also known as the suboblique lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Nephrodium subobliquatum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for skin irritations and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, where it can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from eczema and acne to bronchitis and asthma.
